metadata.summary: Router for six BA trends (2025-2026) with triggers, routing matrix, paste-ready prompts, checklist, commands, and verification gates.

content.title: BA Trends Summary 2025-2026 (Routing Index)
content.summary: Router for six BA trends (AI-enabled decisions, outcome strategy, responsible innovation, resilience, digital trust, sustainability) with concrete triggers, paste-ready prompt, checklist, and verification.

why.p: BA practice is evolving across six identifiable vectors that cut across all traditional knowledge areas. A single index prevents agents from loading every sibling methodology on trend queries and allows rapid routing to the correct downstream methodology for actionable work.
This file is intentionally a router, not a do-er: it owns triggers, routing rules, and a verify gate. The doing lives in the linked methodologies.

list.item: User or agent message contains: "trend", "trending", "2025", "2026", "what's new in BA", "where should I focus", "skill priorities", "what to learn next".
Initial orientation to ba-core before any specific methodology is selected.
Quarterly skill-development planning: pick which 1-2 trends to invest in next.
Triage: a vague stakeholder request mentions AI / ESG / resilience / privacy and you need to decide which methodology owns it.
Actionable BA work is requested (eliciting requirements, writing user stories, building a stakeholder map) — go directly to the linked methodology.
You need prompts, checklists, or scripts for a specific technique — those live in the downstream methodologies, not here.
Detailed framework content (BABOK tasks, BPMN rules, SWOT templates) — load the dedicated methodology instead.
AI-enabled decision making: integrating AI/ML outputs into business decisions and requirements processes. Route to: ai-enabled-business-analysis/.
Outcome-driven strategy: shifting from feature delivery to measurable business outcomes and OKRs. Route to: strategy-methods/, strategy-analysis/.
Responsible innovation: ethical frameworks, bias detection, and governance for AI-driven products. Route to: modern-ba-framework/, ba-governance/.
Operational resilience: continuity planning, failure-mode analysis, and resilience by design. Route to: solution-assessment/, ba-planning/.
Digital trust: data privacy, security requirements, and stakeholder trust as explicit BA concerns. Route to: ba-governance/, requirements-validation/.
Sustainability integration: ESG requirements and sustainability metrics as first-class BA artifacts. Route to: strategy-methods/, modern-ba-framework/.

rule.statement: Match the strongest keyword in the user message against the table; if two trends match, pick the one with the higher routing weight (AI-enabled > digital-trust > outcome-strategy > responsible-innovation > resilience > sustainability).
rule.rationale: Deterministic tie-breaking prevents oscillation between trends and produces reproducible routing across sessions.

list.item: Keywords "AI", "ML", "copilot", "automation outcome", "model output" → ai-enabled-business-analysis.
Keywords "OKR", "outcome", "north-star", "value driver", "KPI tree" → strategy-methods, strategy-analysis.
Keywords "ethics", "bias", "fair", "explainable", "governance board" → modern-ba-framework, ba-governance.
Keywords "resilience", "continuity", "failure mode", "BCP", "DR", "chaos" → solution-assessment, ba-planning.
Keywords "privacy", "GDPR", "DPIA", "trust", "consent", "data residency" → ba-governance, requirements-validation.
Keywords "ESG", "carbon", "sustainability", "scope-3", "green" → strategy-methods, modern-ba-framework.
Data Quality: ~60% of AI projects fail due to poor data — validate data sources before requirements are written.
Business Process Clarity: most enterprise AI fails without solid process documentation.
Stakeholder Alignment: critical for adoption and value realization.
Continuous Learning: technology evolution requires ongoing upskilling across all six trend areas.
Ethical Framework: trust and governance are strategic differentiators, not compliance overhead.

rule.statement: Treat this matrix as a routing aid only; for actionable skill development, go to the methodology linked under each trend.
rule.rationale: Skill priorities are contextual; the matrix tells you where to start, not what to do.

list.item: AI/ML Literacy — Critical, immediate. Route to: ai-enabled-business-analysis/.
Data Analytics — High, immediate. Route to: data-driven-requirements/.
Agile/Enterprise Agile Framework — High, ongoing. Route to: agile-ba-frameworks/.
Process Mining — Medium, mid-term. Route to: process-mining-automation/.
Automation Analysis — Medium, mid-term. Route to: process-mining-automation/.
Strategic Thinking — High, continuous. Route to: strategy-analysis/, strategy-methods/.

how-to.step: Read the user's request and extract the dominant noun phrase (AI, OKR, ethics, resilience, privacy, ESG, etc.).
Look up the phrase in the trigger-to-methodology map above; pick the methodology with the higher routing weight if multiple match.
Stop loading this file. Open the target methodology with Read on `skills/faion/knowledge/pro/ba/ba-core/<target>/methodology.xml`.
If no trend matches, return to ba-core CLAUDE.md and pick by knowledge area instead (planning, elicitation, requirements, strategy, evaluation).
Confirm the routing by stating: "Routing to <slug> because keyword X matched trend Y." This is the verification gate.

checklist.check: Did the user message contain at least one trigger keyword from the map? If no, do not route via this file — use ba-core CLAUDE.md.
Did you pick exactly one primary methodology (not a list)? Multi-route only when the user asks for a comparison.
Did you state the matched keyword and the chosen trend in your response before opening the next file?
Did you avoid loading any sibling methodology that is not in the routing target?
Did you skip emitting prompts, templates, or step-by-step technique content from this file? (That work belongs to the downstream methodology.)

template.p: Lightweight decision record the agent appends to its scratchpad after routing:

example.title: trend-routing-decision-record
example.code:
## Trend Routing Decision
- Date: 2026-05-01 - User asked: "How do we add explainability to our AI loan-decision flow?" - Matched keyword: "explainability" - Trend: responsible-innovation - Primary methodology: modern-ba-framework - Secondary methodology: ba-governance - Confidence: high - Next action: Read modern-ba-framework/methodology.xml; do not load this file again this session.

verify.step: Run: `python3 scripts/validate-methodology-xml.py skills/faion/knowledge/pro/ba/ba-core/ba-trends-summary/methodology.xml` and confirm exit code 0.
Confirm the response contains exactly one primary methodology slug, and that slug appears as a sibling directory under ba-core/.
Confirm no technique-level content (templates, prompts beyond the routing one above, framework rules) was emitted from this file.
Confirm the matched keyword is quoted verbatim from the user message in the response.
If the user follow-up requests technique-level work, switch to the downstream methodology and stop quoting this router.

content.warning: Do not use this file as the source of truth for technique content. It is a router. If the agent finds itself drafting requirements, prompts, or frameworks while this file is open, it has already taken a wrong turn — close it and load the routed methodology instead.
